Deploy

  • Documentation site
RGC Circuit Simulator
  • Model equations
  • View page source

Model equations

All equations are given in LaTeX; the simulator implements these in a discrete, vectorized form on a 2D grid. Parameters are defined in the code (see Parameters (reference)).

Notation

  • \((x,y)\) — spatial position on the retinal patch (e.g. in degrees).

  • \(\lambda\) — wavelength; \(S(x,y,\lambda)\) stimulus spectrum.

  • \(C_L, C_M, C_S\) — L, M, S cone responses; \(C^{\mathrm{eff}}\) — effective cone output after horizontal feedback.

  • \(H\) — horizontal cell activation; \(B\) — bipolar; \(A\) — amacrine; \(G\) — RGC generator; \(R\) — firing rate (sp/s).

  • \(*\) — 2D convolution; \(G_\sigma\) — Gaussian kernel with standard deviation \(\sigma\) (in degrees or pixels).

  • Subscripts ON/OFF and L/M and pathway names (midget, parasol, AII, wide) follow the code.

—

1. Stimulus and cone responses

Stimulus is a spectral grid \(S(x,y,\lambda)\). Cone fundamentals \(\bar{l}(\lambda), \bar{m}(\lambda), \bar{s}(\lambda)\) (normalized) give:

\[C_L(x,y) = \int S(x,y,\lambda)\,\bar{l}(\lambda)\,d\lambda,\quad C_M(x,y) = \int S(x,y,\lambda)\,\bar{m}(\lambda)\,d\lambda,\quad C_S(x,y) = \int S(x,y,\lambda)\,\bar{s}(\lambda)\,d\lambda.\]

In the code this is a discrete dot product over the wavelength axis (einsum).

Pixel-image stimuli

For analytic stimuli (spot, bar, grating, etc.), \(S(x,y,\lambda)\) is factorized into a spatial mask \(M(x,y)\) and a narrowband spectral profile centered at a user-chosen wavelength (with an overall intensity gain). For image stimuli, each pixel’s \((R,G,B)\) is turned into a per-pixel spectrum before the cone inner product.

The default path (rgbtolms) uses tabulated monitor primaries and resampling onto the simulator grid (build_emission_from_rgb / resample_spd_to_spectral_grid in src/simulation/rgb_mapping.py and build_stimulus_spectrum in src/simulation/stimulus/spectral.py). An older Gaussian-basis option is still in the code: three spectra \(b_R, b_G, b_B\) centered near \(610, 540, 450\,\mathrm{nm}\), each normalized to unit peak, with

\[S(x,y,\lambda) \approx R(x,y)\,b_R(\lambda) + G(x,y)\,b_G(\lambda) + B(x,y)\,b_B(\lambda),\]

and \(R,G,B \in [0,1]\). The middle panel of the figure above shows that basis. A global Intensity \(\alpha \in [0,1]\) scales the constructed spectrum.

—

2. Horizontal cell pooling

Horizontal cells pool L+M and S separately with Gaussians \(G_{\sigma_{\mathrm{LM}}}\) and \(G_{\sigma_{\mathrm{S}}}\):

\[H_{\mathrm{LM}} = (C_L + C_M) * G_{\sigma_{\mathrm{LM}}},\qquad H_{\mathrm{S}} = C_S * G_{\sigma_{\mathrm{S}}}.\]

Combined horizontal activation (with weights \(\alpha_{\mathrm{LM}}, \alpha_{\mathrm{S}}\) and optional connectivity weight \(w_{H\to C}\)):

\[H = \alpha_{\mathrm{LM}} w_{H\to C}\, H_{\mathrm{LM}} + \alpha_{\mathrm{S}} w_{H\to C}\, H_{\mathrm{S}}.\]

—

3. Cone effective output (center–surround)

Horizontal feedback is subtracted from cone output:

\[C^{\mathrm{eff}}_L = C_L - \alpha_{\mathrm{LM}} H,\quad C^{\mathrm{eff}}_M = C_M - \alpha_{\mathrm{LM}} H,\quad C^{\mathrm{eff}}_S = C_S - \alpha_{\mathrm{S}} H.\]

(Optional scaling by \(w_{C\to B}\) is applied in the bipolar step in the code.)

—

4. Bipolar cells (ON/OFF split)

Rectification of effective cone output (midget ON/OFF for L and M; optional weight \(w_{C\to B}\)):

\[B_{\mathrm{ON},L} = \max(0,\; w_{C\to B}\, C^{\mathrm{eff}}_L),\quad B_{\mathrm{OFF},L} = \max(0,\; -w_{C\to B}\, C^{\mathrm{eff}}_L),\]

and similarly for M. Diffuse ON/OFF use a pooled signal:

\[P = (C^{\mathrm{eff}}_L + C^{\mathrm{eff}}_M) * G_{\sigma_{\mathrm{diffuse}}},\quad B_{\mathrm{diffuse,ON}} = \max(0, P),\quad B_{\mathrm{diffuse,OFF}} = \max(0, -P).\]

—

5. Amacrine lateral inhibition

AII and wide amacrines pool bipolar (and optionally cone) activity with weights \(w_{B\to A}\):

\[A_{\mathrm{AII}} = \bigl( B_{\mathrm{ON},L} + B_{\mathrm{ON},M} \bigr) * G_{\sigma_{\mathrm{AII}}},\qquad A_{\mathrm{wide}} = \bigl( C^{\mathrm{eff}}_L + C^{\mathrm{eff}}_M \bigr) * G_{\sigma_{\mathrm{wide}}}.\]

Total amacrine inhibition (with \(\gamma_{\mathrm{AII}}, \gamma_{\mathrm{wide}}\) and optional \(w_{A\to B}\)):

\[A_{\mathrm{total}} = w_{A\to B}\,\bigl( \gamma_{\mathrm{AII}} A_{\mathrm{AII}} + \gamma_{\mathrm{wide}} A_{\mathrm{wide}} \bigr).\]

(Code uses \(A_{\mathrm{total}}\) subtracted from bipolar before the dendritic Gaussian.)

—

6. RGC generators (dendritic integration)

Bipolar drive minus amacrine, scaled by \(w_{B\to R}\), is convolved with a Gaussian (dendritic field) to get the generator signal:

\[G = \bigl( B - A_{\mathrm{total}} \bigr) * G_{\sigma_{\mathrm{dend}}}.\]

Here \(B\) is the appropriate bipolar pathway (midget ON/OFF L/M or diffuse ON/OFF); \(\sigma_{\mathrm{dend}}\) is midget or parasol (e.g. \(\sigma_{\mathrm{midget}}\), \(\sigma_{\mathrm{parasol}}\)).

—

7. Firing rate (LN model)

Sigmoid nonlinearity maps generator \(G\) to firing rate \(R\) (sp/s):

\[R = \frac{R_{\max}}{1 + e^{-\beta\,(G - G_{1/2})}}.\]

Parameters: \(R_{\max}\) (max rate), \(G_{1/2}\) (half-max input), \(\beta\) (slope). In the code these are r_max, x_half, slope (see sigmoid_ln in src/simulation/fast_layers.py and use in pipeline.py).

—

8. Color opponent signals

For analysis/display:

\[\mathrm{L{-}M} = R_{\mathrm{ON},L} - R_{\mathrm{ON},M},\qquad \mathrm{S{-}(L{+}M)} = C^{\mathrm{eff}}_S - \tfrac{1}{2}(C^{\mathrm{eff}}_L + C^{\mathrm{eff}}_M).\]

—

9. Temporal smoothing (RC filter)

Each layer is low-pass filtered with a first-order exponential (time constant \(\tau\)). Discrete update (Euler-like, with step \(\Delta t\)):

\[\alpha = \frac{\Delta t}{\tau},\quad \alpha \in [0,1],\qquad X_{t+1} = X_t + \alpha\,(X^{\mathrm{raw}}_{t+1} - X_t).\]

So the stored state \(X\) is the smoothed value; \(X^{\mathrm{raw}}\) is the new value from the feedforward pipeline. Different \(\tau\) are used for cone, horizontal, bipolar, amacrine, and RGC layers.

—

Parameters (reference)

  • Retina: \(\Delta x\) (grid spacing in deg), grid size (e.g. 256×256), field size (e.g. 1°).

  • Horizontal: \(\sigma_{\mathrm{LM}}, \sigma_{\mathrm{S}}\) (deg), \(\alpha_{\mathrm{LM}}, \alpha_{\mathrm{S}}\).

  • Bipolar: \(\sigma_{\mathrm{diffuse}}\) (deg).

  • Amacrine: \(\sigma_{\mathrm{AII}}, \sigma_{\mathrm{wide}}\) (deg), \(\gamma_{\mathrm{AII}}, \gamma_{\mathrm{wide}}\).

  • Dendritic: \(\sigma_{\mathrm{midget}}, \sigma_{\mathrm{parasol}}\) (deg).

  • RGC LN: \(R_{\max}\), \(G_{1/2}\), \(\beta\) (slope).

  • Temporal: \(\tau\) per layer (cone, horizontal, bipolar, amacrine, RGC).

Connectivity weights \(w_{C\to H}, w_{C\to B}, w_{H\to C}, w_{B\to A}, w_{A\to B}, w_{B\to R}\) scale the corresponding pathways (default 1); they are used both in the pipeline and in the 3D connectivity visualization.
